findings.evidence: `Basic.lean:83` marks `blockLaw_apply` as `@[simp]`, and `ConditionallyIID.lean:145` marks the analogous rectangle law `ConditionallyIIDWith.blockLaw_univ_pi` as `@[grind =>]`; the new theorem at `Basic.lean:90` has neither.
findings.fix: Add the appropriate attributes, e.g. `@[simp, grind =>]`, with the rewrite directed from `blockLaw μ X k (Set.univ.pi B)` to the coordinate preimage measure.
findings.issue: `blockLaw_apply_rectangle` is the normal-form evaluator for `blockLaw μ X k (Set.univ.pi B)`, but it has no automation annotation, so downstream users must explicitly `rw` the characteristic API instead of letting `simp`/`grind` reduce rectangle evaluations.

summary: The new rectangle evaluator is the characteristic public API for `blockLaw`, but it is not exposed to the automation conventions used by this file/layer.
